steps.title: Compare the dating methods
steps.content: Text 1 says assigning ages at Sterkfontein is difficult because mixed soil layers and relocated animal bones make common dating approaches unreliable. Text 2 presents cosmogenic nuclide dating of the surrounding breccia as a way to avoid those specific problems.

steps.title: Avoid overstating the response
steps.content: A adds fossil corruption, which neither text claims. B recommends combining the older methods, but Text 2 says its approach avoids their problems. D is inaccurate because the method is applied to the surrounding breccia, not directly to the fossils. The researchers would emphasize that their method fits Sterkfontein's challenges better. The answer is C.
